questions.questionText: In a circle, a line that touches the circle at exactly one point is called a ______.
solution_steps: Recall definitions: secant cuts circle at 2 points, chord joins 2 points on circle.
A line touching at exactly one point is called a tangent.

questions.questionText: A circular clock has a radius of 10 cm. What is the diameter of the clock?
True or False: (1) The center of a circle lies outside the circle. (2) The radius is always twice the diameter.
solution_steps: Statement 1: center lies INSIDE the circle → False.
Statement 2: diameter = 2 × radius, so radius is HALF the diameter → False.

questions.questionText: Fill in the blanks: (1) A straight line joining two points on a circle passing through its center is called the ______. (2) All points on a circle are at the same ______ from its center.
solution_steps: A chord passing through the center = Diameter.
Definition of circle: locus of points equidistant from center → Distance (radius).

questions.questionText: Label the circle parts: Radius, Diameter (through O), Chord (not passing through O), Arc (between two points), Sector (shaded portion).
questions.correctAnswer: Student must label all 5 parts correctly on the diagram.
solution_steps: Radius: from center to any point on circle.
Diameter: any chord passing through center.
Chord: straight line joining two points on circle (not through center).
Arc: curved segment of circle between two points.
Sector: region bounded by two radii and arc.
